Case title: AI conversational interviewing on welfare stigma or political fear
Short description: Compare AI-only, human-only, and human+AI interviewing on sensitive topics to test whether rapport, repair, and ethical pause remain constitutive.
Empirical unit of analysis: Interview transcripts, paradata, and participant feedback across experimental conditions
Candidate roles tested: Situated interlocutor; accountability and labor allocator
Key typology question: Is situated interlocution a distinct role or just generic ethics and protocol management?
Observable artifacts / traces: Repair episodes, follow-up questions, trust ratings, dropout data, note logs
Key outcomes observed: Disclosure depth, rapport, trust, mode effects, downstream interpretive utility
What would weaken / falsify the role cla: AI-only interviewing matches humans on trust, repair, and interpretive usefulness
Main confounds / risks of misreading: Mode effects depend heavily on topic sensitivity and respondent population
Why this case has high leverage: It directly tests whether the human changes the data that come into existence.
collector_notes: Generated from workbook and workbook-linked sources.
